title: Knights of Sidonia
sourceNotes: Manga complete at 78 chapters / 15 volumes (Tsutomu Nihei, 2009-2015). Two anime seasons (Polygon Pictures): S1 'Knights of Sidonia' (12 eps, 2014) and S2 'Battle for Planet Nine' / Daikyuu Wakusei Seneki (12 eps, 2015). Episodes are CUMULATIVE: S2 episodes 1-12 are listed here as 13-24. Together the two seasons adapt roughly chapters 1-41 of the manga; a manga reader continues at chapter 42 after the anime. The 2021 film 'Ai Tsumugu Hoshi' (Love Woven in the Stars) then adapts the remaining final arc (~ch 42-78) in highly abridged and partly altered form. Volume-to-chapter map (Wikipedia): V1 1-5, V2 6-10, V3 11-15, V4 16-20, V5 21-25, V6 26-30, V7 31-35, V8 36-39, V9 40-44, V10 45-49 ... V15 71-78. Episode-to-chapter ranges within each season are approximate. The earlier 2015 'Sidonia no Kishi Movie' (id 21154) is a S1 recap compilation and is excluded.

mappings.arc: Recruitment & First Sorties (Nagate becomes a Garde pilot)
Hoshijiro & the Immortal Crew (Shizuka's loss; Nagate's secret)
Benisuzume / Large Gauna Cluster Ship (S1 climax)
Gravitational Beam Emitter (the experimental weapon backfires)
Battle for Planet Nine / Lem (S2 climax)
Final Arc — Kanata no Kabi & the Gauna War's conclusion (manga only; abridged by the 2021 film)

movies.title: Knights of Sidonia: Love Woven in the Stars (Ai Tsumugu Hoshi)
movies.note: Canonical sequel film concluding the series; abridges and alters the manga's final arc (chapters ~42-78).
